skill.name: developing-genkit-python
skill.description: Guides developers through building Genkit Python apps with flows, tools, prompts, streaming, FastAPI, evaluations, and common fixes.
skill.summary: Build and troubleshoot Genkit Python applications with focused reference guidance.
skill.author: firebase

security_audit.summary: Static analysis raised many command, network, filesystem, and environment findings, but most are Markdown examples for normal Genkit development rather than executable skill behavior. The main confirmed risks are documentation patterns that tell users to persist an API key in a shell profile and to install uv with a pipe-to-shell command, so publication is acceptable with a warning.

medium_findings.title: Pipe-to-Shell Installer Command in Documentation
medium_findings.description: The troubleshooting table recommends running a remote uv installer through a shell pipeline. This is a known risky installation pattern because users execute remote script content without local review, although the URL is an official Astral installer and no malicious destination was found.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The risky command pattern is directly present on the cited line. Confidence is below very high because it points to a legitimate installer rather than an unknown or clearly malicious endpoint.

medium_findings.title: API Key Persistence in Shell Profile
medium_findings.description: The workflow suggests appending a Gemini API key export to a user shell profile. This can leave long-lived credentials on disk and may expose them through backups or shared workstation profiles, but it is presented as optional setup guidance rather than hidden credential collection.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The filesystem write and environment variable are explicit in the documentation. The risk is moderate because it stores a placeholder key locally and does not transmit credentials to another service.

low_findings.title: Markdown Command Examples Flagged as Code Execution
low_findings.description: The static analyzer reported many Ruby or shell backtick execution findings, but reviewed examples are Markdown inline code or fenced developer commands for Genkit usage. No evidence found that the skill itself executes these commands automatically.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations are documentation text or code examples, not executable skill scripts. Multiple reviewed files follow the same reference-documentation pattern.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs Are Documentation and Localhost References
low_findings.description: The network findings point to official setup documentation, local development UI URLs, localhost curl examples, and local Dev UI instructions. No evidence found of exfiltration endpoints or background network calls.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The destinations are visible and consistent with setup or localhost testing. The reviewed context does not show secret transmission to external endpoints.

low_findings.title: Weak Crypto Static Finding Appears Unsubstantiated
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged a weak cryptographic algorithm at the skill description line, but that line is plain metadata describing Genkit Python usage. No evidence found of cryptographic operations at that location.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line contains only natural-language metadata. There is no cryptographic API, algorithm name, or security-sensitive computation in that context.

dangerous_patterns.title: Remote Installer Piped to Shell
dangerous_patterns.description: Documentation includes a pipe-to-shell installer command. Users should verify installer scripts or use package-manager alternatives when possible.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The exact pipe-to-shell pattern is present. It is dangerous as a general pattern even when the referenced installer is likely legitimate.

dangerous_patterns.title: Credential Export Written to Shell Profile
dangerous_patterns.description: Documentation includes a command that appends an API key export to a shell startup file. This can persist secrets longer than intended.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command explicitly writes an environment variable to a hidden shell profile. It uses a placeholder value and remains a user-run documentation step.

content.value_statement: Genkit Python changes quickly, so developers can lose time on outdated imports and API shapes. This skill gives focused references for flows, prompts, tools, FastAPI, evaluations, and troubleshooting.
actual_capabilities: Explains Genkit Python project setup with uv, virtual environments, and Google AI plugins.
Provides examples for flows, tools, structured output, streaming, embeddings, and media responses.
Shows FastAPI integration patterns for Genkit flow endpoints and streaming responses.
Documents dotprompt file structure, schema registration, variants, partials, and helper functions.
Covers Genkit evaluation setup with built-in evaluators, custom evaluators, datasets, and CLI runs.
Lists common Genkit Python errors with targeted fixes for imports, model IDs, event loops, and streaming.
limitations: It is reference guidance, not an automated code generator or test runner.
It assumes users can install Python 3.14, uv, Node-based Genkit CLI, and provider plugins.
Examples focus on Google AI and Gemini model identifiers, with only brief notes for other providers.
Some setup commands require users to manage API keys and local environment security carefully.

use_cases.title: Start a Genkit Python App
use_cases.description: Create a new Python project, add Genkit dependencies, configure a Gemini API key, and run a first flow.

use_cases.title: Serve AI Flows with FastAPI
use_cases.description: Connect Genkit flows to FastAPI routes and support streaming responses for browser or curl clients.

use_cases.title: Debug Genkit SDK Issues
use_cases.description: Resolve common import, model ID, event loop, schema, and streaming errors using focused references.

prompt_templates.title: Create a First Flow
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for a small Genkit Python app.
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me create a minimal Genkit Python app with one flow. Use the skill references and explain each setup step.

prompt_templates.title: Fix a Genkit Error
prompt_templates.scenario: Troubleshooting a known SDK or runtime problem.
prompt_templates.prompt: I am seeing this Genkit Python error: [paste error]. Find the likely cause and give the smallest safe fix.

prompt_templates.title: Add Streaming FastAPI Output
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert my Genkit Python flow to a FastAPI endpoint that streams output. Include the required handler and client expectations.

prompt_templates.title: Design an Evaluated Agent Flow
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced Genkit workflow with prompts and evaluation.
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a Genkit Python agent flow with tools, dotprompt files, structured output, and an evaluation plan.

output_examples.input: I need a Genkit Python project that uses Gemini and returns structured city data.
output_examples.output: A setup plan with uv project steps, required Genkit packages, the Google AI plugin, a Pydantic schema, and a flow testing checklist.

output_examples.input: My FastAPI Genkit endpoint is not streaming in the browser.
output_examples.output: A diagnosis covering the event-stream Accept header, ActionRunContext usage, chunk forwarding, decorator order, and Dev UI verification steps.

output_examples.input: I want to evaluate whether my generated answers match expected references.
output_examples.output: An evaluation approach using a dataset, built-in regex evaluator options, a custom evaluator outline, and commands to run results in the Dev UI.

best_practices: Verify imports and API shapes against the included references before relying on memory.
Keep API keys in local environment tooling and avoid committing them to project files.
Use Pydantic models for tool inputs and structured outputs to match Genkit and Gemini expectations.
anti_patterns: Do not use internal Genkit packages when public modules are available.
Do not use bare scalar tool parameters when Gemini requires object schemas.
Do not call asyncio.run for long-lived apps started through genkit start.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps build, serve, evaluate, and troubleshoot Genkit applications written in Python.

faq.question: Which provider do the examples use?
faq.answer: The examples primarily use Google AI with Gemini model identifiers and the Google GenAI plugin.

faq.question: Does it include FastAPI guidance?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes FastAPI route patterns, streaming response behavior, decorator order, and local testing guidance.

faq.question: Can it help with dotprompt files?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers prompt file format, schemas, variants, partials, helpers, and rendering prompts before generation.

faq.question: Does it run commands automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides documentation and command examples for users to run in their own development environment.

faq.question: What security care is needed?
faq.answer: Review installer commands before running them and avoid storing real API keys in shared or committed files.
